matches.rationale: The Bash rule triggers on pull-request creation after library code changes and requires reading the library pre-command guidance, so it covers the same pre-PR formatting obligation. Its extracted pattern/evidence does not specifically identify `make format`, and the same umbrella rule also covers linting and testing; under the 1:1 rule it is assigned only to g1, with those command-specific clauses omitted.

The Edit rule detects Sphinx-style double-backtick inline-code text in Python edits, exactly targeting the prohibited formatting in docstrings and comments. Its path/pattern approximation does not itself distinguish those syntactic contexts, but the evidence states the same target.

misses.note: The only library pre-PR Bash extraction is the umbrella rule `lib-pr-precommands`, already consumed by the closest available 1:1 match; no separate extracted rule targets `make lint`.
The only library pre-PR Bash extraction is the umbrella rule `lib-pr-precommands`, already consumed by the closest available 1:1 match; no separate extracted rule targets `make test`.
No extracted Bash rule targets use of the `TEST` variable to select a test file or pass additional pytest arguments.

The sole Edit extraction combines the double-backtick prohibition with the single-backtick recommendation and is already matched to the more direct double-backtick rule g5; 1:1 matching leaves no distinct extracted rule for the positive single-backtick requirement.
